items.title: Apple announces Apple Intelligence powered accessibility feature updates
items.content: Apple is introducing a feature to let Vision Pro control a compatible wheelchair with their eyes

items.title: Apple’s Siri revamp could include auto-deleting chats
items.content: Privacy will be a major theme when Apple unveils a new version of Siri.

items.title: Spotify to adopt Apple’s new video podcast tech, offering creators easier cross-platform distribution
items.content: Spotify will let creators distribute and monetize video podcasts on Apple Podcasts using Apple’s HLS streaming technology, without changing their existing workflows.

items.title: Ransomware hackers claim breach at Foxconn, a major electronics manufacturer for Apple, Google, and Nvidia
items.content: A ransomware group has claimed responsibility for hacking the electronics manufacturing giant Foxconn and is attempting to extort the company.

items.title: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI
items.content: Elon Musk's claim that he was mistreated by his OpenAI co-founders failed after nine California jurors decided in a unanimous verdict that his lawsuits had been filed too late

items.title: Microsoft’s AI data center push is colliding with its clean power goals
items.content: The push for new data centers at Microsoft is putting its key clean power goals at risk.

items.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
items.content: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models

items.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
items.content: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets a

items.title: Rising Energy Costs and Data Centers at Heart of NextEra’s Dominion Bid
items.content: NextEra’s proposed acquisition of Dominion Energy comes as Americans are paying a lot more for electricity, and data centers are demanding a lot more power from utilities.

items.title: Amazon E-Bikes Take Root in Manhattan and Brooklyn
items.content: Small companies that handle the final delivery are relying on them to skirt New York City traffic. Amazon plans to try the bikes elsewhere, too.

items.title: China Wants A.I. to Flourish, but Not at the Expense of Jobs
items.content: A series of precedent-setting rulings signals that Chinese courts are being enlisted to shield workers from displacement by artificial intelligence.

items.title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
items.content: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established man
